segments.beat: hand holds device face-on, small OLED screen lit, transparent shell shows PCB underneath
device rotated and tilted, clear shell and internal battery/board fully visible from multiple angles
device set flat on surface, front-facing stable shot showing full PCB layout through shell
hand picks device back up, angled view continues to expose internals and lit screen
device tilted toward camera, rear PCB components and wiring clearly visible through resin
held in palm, final front-facing frame — controls, screen, and internals all visible simultaneously

demonstrates.motion_value: high — continuous rotation reveals all internal layers (PCB, battery, wiring) through clear shell
medium — OLED lit and legible against transparent pane in opening frame
medium — hand-held throughout, device clearly palm-sized
medium — stable flat lay and close angles show clean resin finish and tidy PCB layout
low — flat-lay framing has display-object quality but no deliberate collectible staging
low — rear PCB and wiring visible but not explicitly demonstrated

selection_note: Continuous rotation over 6s delivers the single strongest proof of the load-bearing transparency claim; OLED-on in opening frame doubles as see_through_display_on; clean loop candidate.

aesthetics.weakness: handheld prototype feel - exposed wires, battery, PCB hurt consumer credibility
aesthetics.note: flat ambient lighting, inconsistent orientation, raw dev-kit aesthetic; needs a lightbox/table shoot
